title: Spaghetti Carbonara
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti and cook according to package instructions until al dente, usually about 8–10 minutes.
While the pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add finely chopped pancetta or bacon and cook until crispy and golden, about 5–6 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, grated Parmesan cheese, and a generous amount of black pepper until the mixture becomes smooth and creamy.
When the pasta is done, reserve about 1/2 cup of the pasta cooking water, then drain the spaghetti.
Add the hot pasta directly into the skillet with the pancetta. Toss well so the pasta is coated in the flavorful oil.
Remove the skillet from heat and slowly pour the egg and cheese mixture over the pasta while continuously stirring. The residual heat from the pasta will cook the eggs and create a creamy sauce.
If the sauce appears too thick, add a small amount of the reserved pasta water and stir until it reaches a silky consistency.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and additional black pepper if needed.
Serve immediately topped with extra Parmesan cheese and freshly chopped parsley.

title: Chicken Fajitas
steps: Begin by slicing the chicken breasts into thin strips. This allows the chicken to cook quickly and evenly.

Place the chicken strips in a mixing bowl and add chili powder, paprika, cumin, salt, black pepper, minced garlic, olive oil, and the juice of half a lime. Mix thoroughly so the chicken is well coated in the seasoning.
Cover the bowl and let the chicken marinate for at least 15–20 minutes. If you have more time, marinating for up to an hour will enhance the flavor.
While the chicken marinates, slice the bell peppers and onion into thin strips.
Heat a large skillet or cast iron pan over medium-high heat and add a small amount of olive oil.
Add the marinated chicken to the hot pan and cook for about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally until the chicken is fully cooked and slightly browned.
Remove the cooked chicken from the skillet and set it aside temporarily.
In the same skillet, add the sliced bell peppers and onions. Cook for about 5 minutes until the vegetables soften slightly but still retain some crunch.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and mix it with the vegetables. Cook together for another 2–3 minutes so the flavors combine.
Warm the flour tortillas either in a dry pan for about 30 seconds on each side or in the microwave for 15–20 seconds.
To assemble the fajitas, place a portion of the chicken and vegetable mixture in the center of each tortilla.
Top with sour cream, guacamole, chopped cilantro, and an extra squeeze of fresh lime juice if desired.
Fold the tortilla around the filling and serve immediately while hot.

title: Chicken Ramen
steps: Start by preparing the broth. In a medium pot, heat sesame oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and grated ginger and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chicken thighs to the pot and lightly sear them for 2–3 minutes on each side to develop flavor.
Pour in the chicken broth and soy sauce. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the broth simmer for about 20 minutes so the chicken becomes tender and the flavors develop.
While the broth cooks, boil eggs in a separate pot for 7 minutes to achieve a slightly soft yolk. Once done, transfer them to cold water, peel them, and slice them in half.
Remove the cooked chicken thighs from the broth and shred the meat using two forks.
Add sliced mushrooms to the broth and cook for another 5 minutes.
In another pot, cook the ramen noodles according to package instructions, usually about 3–4 minutes.
Drain the noodles and divide them evenly into serving bowls.
Pour the hot broth and mushrooms over the noodles.
Top each bowl with shredded chicken, halved boiled eggs, chopped green onions, and a small piece of nori.
Season with salt and black pepper if needed and serve immediately while hot.

title: Paneer Butter Masala
steps: Begin by roughly chopping the tomatoes, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent.
Add garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for another 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften completely and begin to break down.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool slightly. Transfer it to a blender and blend until smooth to create a rich gravy base.
Heat the remaining butter in a clean pan and pour in the blended tomato mixture.
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Stir well and cook for about 5 minutes until the gravy thickens.
Add cashew paste and mix thoroughly to make the gravy creamy and rich.
Pour in the fresh cream and stir gently while cooking on low heat.
Add paneer cubes to the gravy and simmer for about 5 minutes so the paneer absorbs the flavors.
Sprinkle garam masala and a small amount of sugar to balance the acidity of the tomatoes.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves and a small drizzle of cream before serving.
Serve hot with naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice.

title: Classic Pancakes
steps: Start by preparing the dry ingredients. In a large mixing bowl combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix well so the ingredients are evenly distributed.
In another bowl whisk together the milk, egg, melted butter, and vanilla extract until the mixture becomes smooth.
Slowly pour the wet mixture into the bowl with the dry ingredients. Stir gently using a whisk or spatula until a smooth batter forms. Avoid overmixing because that can make the pancakes dense.
Let the batter rest for about 5 minutes. This allows the baking powder to activate and makes the pancakes fluffier.
Heat a non-stick skillet or frying pan over medium heat. Add a small amount of butter and allow it to melt.
Pour about 1/4 cup of pancake batter onto the skillet for each pancake.
Cook for about 2–3 minutes until small bubbles start appearing on the surface and the edges begin to look set.
Carefully flip the pancake using a spatula and cook the other side for another 2 minutes until golden brown.
Repeat the process with the remaining batter, adding a little butter to the pan if needed.
Stack the pancakes on a serving plate while they are still warm.
Serve with maple syrup, a small piece of butter, and optional toppings like fresh berries or sliced bananas.

title: Thai Green Curry Chicken
steps: Start by cooking the jasmine rice according to the package instructions so it will be ready when the curry is finished.
Cut the chicken breast into bite-sized pieces so they cook quickly and evenly.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the green curry paste to the pan and cook for about 1–2 minutes while stirring continuously until the paste becomes aromatic.
Pour in about half of the coconut milk and stir well to combine it with the curry paste.
Add the chicken pieces to the pan and cook for about 5 minutes until the chicken starts turning white on the outside.
Add the remaining coconut milk and stir to combine everything into a smooth curry sauce.
Add sliced bell peppers, broccoli florets, and kaffir lime leaves to the curry.
Season the curry with fish sauce, brown sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Let the curry simmer gently for about 10 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked and the vegetables are tender.
Taste the curry and adjust seasoning if needed by adding a little more fish sauce or sugar.
Add fresh basil leaves right before turning off the heat so they release their aroma into the curry.
Serve the hot green curry over steamed jasmine rice.

title: Vegetable Lo Mein
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the lo mein noodles according to package instructions until they are tender but still slightly firm. Drain the noodles and set them aside.
While the noodles cook, prepare all the vegetables. Slice the carrot and bell pepper into thin strips, shred the cabbage, and trim the snap peas.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the carrots, bell peppers, cabbage, and snap peas to the wok. Stir-fry the vegetables for about 3–4 minutes until they begin to soften but still remain slightly crisp.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, sesame oil, and a pinch of black pepper to create the sauce.
Add the cooked noodles into the wok with the vegetables and toss everything together.
Pour the prepared sauce over the noodles and stir continuously so that the noodles are evenly coated.
Continue cooking for another 2–3 minutes so the noodles absorb the sauce and the flavors combine well.
Add chopped green onions and toss once more.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt or soy sauce if needed.
Serve hot directly from the wok.

title: Greek Chicken Gyro
steps: Begin by preparing the chicken marinade. In a bowl mix olive oil, minced garlic, oregano, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Slice the chicken breasts into thin strips and coat them thoroughly with the marinade.
Cover the bowl and allow the chicken to marinate in the refrigerator for at least 20 minutes so the flavors can develop.
While the chicken marinates, prepare the tzatziki sauce by mixing Greek yogurt, lemon juice, chopped dill, grated cucumber, and a pinch of salt in a bowl.
Slice the tomato and red onion thinly and set them aside.
Heat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat and add a small amount of olive oil.
Cook the marinated chicken strips for about 6–8 minutes, stirring occasionally until they are fully cooked and slightly browned.
Warm the pita breads in a dry pan for about 30 seconds on each side or until soft.
To assemble the gyro, place warm chicken in the center of the pita bread.
Add shredded lettuce, sliced tomato, cucumber, and red onion on top of the chicken.
Drizzle the prepared tzatziki sauce generously over the filling.
Fold the pita bread around the filling to form the gyro wrap.
Serve immediately while warm.

title: Spanish Seafood Paella
steps: Start by heating olive oil in a wide paella pan or large skillet over medium heat.
Finely chop the onion and garlic, then sauté them in the pan for about 3 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Grate or finely chop the tomato and add it to the pan. Cook the mixture for about 5 minutes until it thickens and becomes slightly caramelized.
Add smoked paprika and saffron threads to the pan, stirring gently to release their flavor.
Add the rice to the pan and stir for about 1 minute so that the grains are coated in the flavorful oil.
Pour in the seafood or chicken broth and stir to distribute the rice evenly across the pan.
Allow the mixture to simmer gently without stirring too much, which helps form the traditional paella texture.
After about 10 minutes of cooking, add the shrimp, mussels, and green peas evenly over the rice.
Continue cooking for another 10–12 minutes until the rice has absorbed most of the liquid and the seafood is fully cooked.
Taste the rice and adjust seasoning with salt and black pepper if necessary.
Remove the pan from heat and allow the paella to rest for about 5 minutes.
Garnish with freshly chopped parsley and lemon wedges before serving.
Serve directly from the pan for a traditional presentation.

title: Chicken Alfredo Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the fettuccine pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, season the chicken breasts with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat and add the chicken breasts.
Cook the chicken for about 6–7 minutes per side until golden brown and fully cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pan and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In the same skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the heavy cream and bring it to a gentle simmer while stirring continuously.
Add the grated Parmesan cheese slowly while stirring so that it melts smoothly into the sauce.
Allow the sauce to cook for about 3–4 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Drain the cooked pasta and add it directly to the Alfredo sauce.
Toss the pasta well so that every strand is coated in the creamy sauce.
Add the sliced chicken back into the skillet and mix gently.
Taste the dish and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if needed.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and extra Parmesan cheese before serving.
Serve hot immediately.

title: Bibimbap
steps: Begin by cooking the short-grain rice according to the package instructions and keep it warm until serving.
Slice the carrot and zucchini into thin matchstick pieces so they cook quickly and evenly.
Blanch the spinach in boiling water for about 30 seconds, then transfer it to cold water and squeeze out excess moisture.
Season the spinach lightly with sesame oil, sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt.
Heat a pan with a small amount of vegetable oil and sauté the carrots for about 2 minutes until slightly tender but still crisp.
In the same pan cook the zucchini slices for about 2 minutes with a pinch of salt, then remove and set aside.
Mix the beef strips with soy sauce, minced garlic, sugar, and a few drops of sesame oil.
Cook the marinated beef in a hot skillet for about 3–4 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Fry the egg in a separate pan until the whites are set but the yolk remains slightly runny.
Place the warm rice in a bowl and arrange the cooked vegetables and beef neatly on top in separate sections.
Add the fried egg in the center of the bowl.
Drizzle gochujang over the ingredients and sprinkle sesame seeds on top.
Before eating, mix everything together thoroughly so the rice, vegetables, meat, and sauce combine into a flavorful dish.

title: Ratatouille
steps: Begin by washing all the vegetables thoroughly to remove any dirt or residue.
Slice the eggplant, zucchini, and yellow squash into thin round slices of equal thickness.
Cut the bell pepper into thin strips and finely chop the onion.
Dice the tomatoes into small cubes while keeping their juices.
Heat one tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté for about 3 minutes until it becomes soft and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes along with thyme, oregano, salt, and black pepper to form the base sauce.
Allow the tomato mixture to simmer for about 8 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Spread the tomato sauce evenly in the bottom of a baking dish.
Arrange the sliced eggplant, zucchini, and yellow squash in alternating layers over the sauce.
Drizzle the remaining olive oil evenly across the vegetables.
Cover the dish with foil and bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for about 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for another 10 minutes so the vegetables become slightly roasted.
Garnish the finished ratatouille with freshly chopped basil before serving.

title: Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
steps: Drain the canned chickpeas in a colander and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Allow the chickpeas to sit for a minute so the excess water drains away completely.
Dice the cucumber into small bite-sized cubes and place them into a large mixing bowl.
Slice the cherry tomatoes in half and add them to the bowl with the cucumber.
Peel the red onion and slice it very thinly so the flavor is not overpowering.
Add the sliced onions to the bowl along with the vegetables.
Roughly chop the olives and crumble the feta cheese into small pieces.
Add the chickpeas, olives, and feta cheese into the vegetable mixture.
In a separate small bowl whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, salt, and black pepper to create the dressing.
Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients.
Gently toss everything together using a large spoon so the dressing coats all ingredients evenly.
Chop fresh parsley finely and sprinkle it over the salad.
Taste the salad and adjust seasoning with extra salt or lemon juice if needed.
Let the salad rest for about 10 minutes before serving so the flavors combine.

title: Masala Dosa
steps: Begin by washing the potatoes thoroughly and placing them in a pressure cooker or pot with enough water to cover them.
Boil the potatoes until they become soft and easily mashable, which usually takes about 10–12 minutes.
Once cooked, allow the potatoes to cool slightly, peel off the skins, and mash them roughly using a fork.
Finely chop the onion, green chilies, and fresh coriander leaves and keep them aside.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and add mustard seeds, allowing them to splutter.
Add curry leaves and chopped green chilies to the pan and sauté briefly until aromatic.
Add the chopped onions and cook for about 3–4 minutes until they become soft and slightly translucent.
Stir in turmeric powder and salt, then add the mashed potatoes into the pan.
Mix everything thoroughly so the spices coat the potatoes evenly.
Cook the potato mixture for about 5 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add chopped coriander leaves, mix well, and remove the filling from heat.
Heat a flat non-stick dosa pan or tawa over medium heat and lightly grease it with butter or ghee.
Pour a ladle of dosa batter onto the pan and spread it in a circular motion to form a thin crepe.
Drizzle a little butter or ghee around the edges and cook until the dosa becomes golden and crisp.
Place a portion of the potato masala in the center of the dosa and fold it over before serving.

title: Palak Paneer
steps: Begin by thoroughly washing the spinach leaves under running water to remove any dirt.
Bring a pot of water to a boil and blanch the spinach leaves for about 2 minutes.
Immediately transfer the spinach to cold water to preserve its bright green color.
Drain the spinach and blend it into a smooth puree using a blender.
Finely chop the onion, tomatoes, garlic, and ginger and keep them ready for cooking.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and add cumin seeds, allowing them to splutter.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn soft and lightly golden.
Add ginger and garlic and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they become soft and form a thick mixture.
Stir in turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, and salt.
Pour in the spinach puree and mix it well with the masala base.
Cook the spinach gravy for about 5 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add paneer cubes to the pan and gently mix so they are coated in the spinach sauce.
Add a small amount of water if the gravy becomes too thick.
Stir in fresh cream and cook for another 2 minutes before turning off the heat.
Serve hot with roti, naan, or steamed rice.

title: Aloo Gobi
steps: Wash the potatoes and cauliflower thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities.
Peel the potatoes and cut them into medium-sized cubes.
Cut the cauliflower into small florets of roughly equal size so they cook evenly.
Finely chop the onion, tomatoes, garlic, and ginger and keep them ready.
Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and allow them to splutter for a few seconds.
Add the chopped onions and sauté for about 4 minutes until they turn soft and slightly golden.
Add minced garlic and ginger and cook for another minute until aromatic.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften and form a thick masala base.
Stir in turmeric powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, and salt.
Add the diced potatoes to the pan and mix well so they get coated with the spices.
Cover the pan and cook the potatoes for about 5 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add the cauliflower florets and mix them gently with the potato mixture.
Cover again and cook for another 10–12 minutes until both vegetables become tender.
Sprinkle garam masala over the dish and mix lightly.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves before serving.

title: Butter Chicken
steps: Start by washing the chicken pieces thoroughly and cutting them into medium-sized cubes.
In a bowl combine yogurt, turmeric powder, chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add the chicken pieces into the marinade and mix well so the chicken is evenly coated.
Cover the bowl and allow the chicken to marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Finely chop the onions, tomatoes, garlic, and ginger while the chicken marinates.
Heat oil and one tablespoon of butter in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn golden brown and soft.
Add minced garlic and ginger and cook for about one minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften and form a thick sauce.
Add the marinated chicken into the pan and mix well with the tomato mixture.
Cook the chicken for about 10–12 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add a little water if the gravy becomes too thick and continue simmering.
Stir in fresh cream and the remaining butter to make the sauce rich and smooth.
Sprinkle garam masala and mix gently to combine the flavors.
Cook for another 3 minutes on low heat so the chicken absorbs the creamy sauce.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves before serving.

title: Pav Bhaji
steps: Wash the potatoes, cauliflower, and green peas thoroughly before cooking.
Boil the potatoes, cauliflower, and peas together in a pressure cooker until they become soft.
Once cooked, mash the vegetables roughly using a potato masher and set them aside.
Finely chop the onion, tomatoes, capsicum, garlic, and ginger.
Heat oil and one tablespoon of butter in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they become soft and slightly golden.
Add garlic and ginger and cook for about one minute until aromatic.
Add the chopped capsicum and cook for another two minutes while stirring.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a thick mixture.
Mix in turmeric powder, chili powder, pav bhaji masala, and salt.
Add the mashed vegetables to the pan and mix thoroughly with the masala base.
Add a little water and mash the mixture further so the bhaji becomes smooth.
Cook the bhaji for about 8 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add the remaining butter and mix it well into the curry.
Toast the pav buns on a hot pan with a little butter until golden brown.
Garnish the bhaji with fresh coriander leaves and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve the hot bhaji with buttered pav buns.

title: Chicken Chettinad
steps: Begin by washing the chicken pieces thoroughly and draining any excess water.
Finely chop the onions and tomatoes and keep them ready for cooking.
Crush the garlic and ginger into a paste using a mortar or blender.
In a dry pan roast fennel seeds, black pepper, coriander seeds, and dried red chilies until aromatic.
Allow the roasted spices to cool slightly and grind them into a coarse powder.
Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat and add mustard seeds.
When the mustard seeds start spluttering, add fresh curry leaves.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they become soft and lightly golden.
Add the ginger garlic paste and cook for about one minute until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a thick masala base.
Stir in turmeric powder and salt and mix well with the masala.
Add the chicken pieces and cook for about 5 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add the freshly ground Chettinad spice mixture and mix thoroughly.
Pour a small amount of water into the pan and cover it with a lid.
Allow the chicken to simmer for about 15 minutes until tender and fully cooked.
Sprinkle garam masala over the curry and stir gently.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves before serving.

title: Chicken Teriyaki
steps: Start by trimming any excess fat from the chicken thighs and pat them dry using paper towels.
Lightly season the chicken with a small amount of salt and black pepper on both sides.
In a small bowl combine soy sauce, mirin, sugar, and honey to prepare the teriyaki sauce.
Stir the sauce mixture well until the sugar dissolves completely.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Place the chicken thighs skin-side down in the pan and cook for about 5 minutes until the skin becomes golden and slightly crispy.
Flip the chicken and cook the other side for another 4–5 minutes until nearly cooked through.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger into the pan and sauté briefly until fragrant.
Pour the prepared teriyaki sauce into the skillet with the chicken.
Allow the sauce to simmer and thicken while spooning it repeatedly over the chicken.
Cook for about 4 minutes until the sauce becomes glossy and coats the chicken evenly.
Remove the chicken from the pan and slice it into thin strips.
Serve the sliced chicken over a bowl of warm steamed rice.
Drizzle the remaining teriyaki sauce from the pan over the chicken.
Garnish with chopped green onions and sesame seeds before serving.

title: Beef Tacos
steps: Begin by finely chopping the onion, garlic, and tomato and keeping them ready for cooking.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook for about 3 minutes until they become soft.
Add minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the ground beef into the skillet and break it apart using a spatula.
Cook the beef for about 6–7 minutes until it becomes browned and fully cooked.
Drain any excess fat from the skillet if necessary.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook for about 2 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in chili powder, paprika, cumin powder, salt, and black pepper.
Mix everything thoroughly so the beef becomes evenly coated with the spices.
Add a small splash of water and simmer for about 3 minutes to blend the flavors.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry pan for about 20 seconds on each side.
Place a portion of the cooked beef mixture in the center of each tortilla.
Top the tacos with shredded lettuce and shredded cheese.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of fresh lime juice before serving.

title: Margherita Pizza
steps: Begin by preheating the oven to 220°C so it is hot enough for baking the pizza.
Lightly dust a clean surface with flour and place the pizza dough on it.
Roll the dough gently using a rolling pin until it forms a thin circular base.
Transfer the rolled dough onto a baking tray or pizza stone.
Spread pizza sauce evenly over the surface of the dough leaving a small border around the edges.
Thinly slice the fresh mozzarella cheese and distribute the slices evenly across the pizza.
Slice the tomato into thin rounds and place them on top of the cheese.
Finely chop the garlic and sprinkle it lightly over the pizza for extra flavor.
Drizzle olive oil across the pizza surface to help it bake evenly.
Season with dried oregano, chili flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Place the pizza tray in the preheated oven and bake for about 12–15 minutes.
Check occasionally until the crust becomes golden and the cheese melts completely.
Remove the pizza from the oven and allow it to cool slightly for about 2 minutes.
Top the pizza with fresh basil leaves for a classic Margherita flavor.
Slice the pizza into wedges and serve hot.

title: Pad Thai
steps: Start by soaking the rice noodles in warm water for about 15 minutes until they become soft.
Peel and clean the shrimp, removing the veins and rinsing them under cold water.
Finely chop the garlic and slice the green onions into small pieces.
Julienne the carrot into thin strips so it cooks quickly in the stir-fry.
In a small bowl mix fish sauce, tamarind paste, and brown sugar to create the Pad Thai sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped garlic and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the shrimp and cook for about 3–4 minutes until they turn pink and opaque.
Push the shrimp to one side of the wok and crack the eggs into the empty space.
Scramble the eggs gently until they are fully cooked.
Add the softened rice noodles into the wok and toss them with the shrimp and eggs.
Pour the prepared Pad Thai sauce over the noodles and mix well.
Add the carrots and bean sprouts and stir-fry for another 2 minutes.
Sprinkle chopped green onions and crushed roasted peanuts over the noodles.
Serve the Pad Thai hot with lime wedges and fresh cilantro on top.

title: Kung Pao Chicken
steps: Start by cutting the chicken breast into small bite-sized cubes.
Place the chicken in a bowl and mix it with cornstarch, a pinch of salt, and black pepper.
Allow the chicken to marinate for about 10 minutes while preparing the other ingredients.
Chop the red and green bell peppers into small square pieces.
Finely chop the garlic and ginger so they release maximum flavor while cooking.
In a small bowl mix soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, and sugar to prepare the sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the dried red chilies and stir-fry them briefly until aromatic.
Add the chopped garlic and ginger and cook for about 30 seconds.
Add the marinated chicken cubes and stir-fry for about 5–6 minutes until they are cooked through.
Add the chopped bell peppers and continue stir-frying for another 2 minutes.
Pour the prepared sauce into the wok and mix it with the chicken and vegetables.
Allow the sauce to cook for about 2 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Add the roasted peanuts and mix them into the dish.
Garnish with chopped green onions before serving.

title: Moussaka
steps: Begin by washing the eggplants and slicing them into thin round pieces.
Sprinkle a little salt over the eggplant slices and allow them to rest for about 10 minutes to remove bitterness.
Rinse the eggplants lightly and pat them dry using paper towels.
Heat a pan with a small amount of olive oil and lightly fry the eggplant slices until they turn slightly golden.
Remove the eggplants from the pan and place them on a plate lined with paper towels.
Finely chop the onion, garlic, and tomatoes and keep them ready for cooking.
Heat olive oil in another pan and sauté the chopped onions until soft.
Add garlic and cook for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the ground lamb and cook while breaking it apart until browned.
Add chopped tomatoes, cinnamon, oregano, salt, and black pepper and allow the mixture to simmer for about 10 minutes.
In a separate saucepan melt butter over medium heat and add flour to create a roux.
Slowly pour in the milk while whisking continuously to form a smooth béchamel sauce.
Cook the sauce until it thickens, then remove it from heat and mix in the egg and grated cheese.
Layer the fried eggplants and the lamb mixture in a baking dish.
Pour the béchamel sauce evenly over the top layer.
Bake the dish in a preheated oven at 180°C for about 30 minutes until the top becomes golden.
Allow the moussaka to rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.

title: Spanish Tortilla
steps: Begin by peeling the potatoes and slicing them into thin round slices so they cook evenly.
Peel the onion and slice it into thin strips.
Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the sliced potatoes and cook slowly for about 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add the sliced onions to the pan and continue cooking until both potatoes and onions become soft.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper.
Remove the cooked potatoes and onions from the oil and allow excess oil to drain.
Crack the eggs into a large bowl and whisk them until fully combined.
Add the cooked potato and onion mixture into the eggs and gently mix.
Allow the mixture to sit for about 5 minutes so the potatoes absorb the egg.
Heat a small amount of oil in a clean pan over medium heat.
Pour the potato and egg mixture into the pan and spread it evenly.
Cook the tortilla for about 5 minutes until the bottom becomes firm.
Carefully flip the tortilla using a plate and cook the other side for another 4 minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley before serving.

title: Bulgogi
steps: Start by placing the thinly sliced beef in a large mixing bowl.
Finely mince the garlic and grate the ginger so they blend easily into the marinade.
In a separate bowl combine soy sauce, sesame oil, brown sugar, honey, minced garlic, and grated ginger.
Mix the marinade thoroughly until the sugar dissolves completely.
Pour the marinade over the sliced beef and mix well so every piece is coated.
Cover the bowl and allow the beef to marinate for at least 20 minutes.
While the beef marinates, slice the onion, carrot, and green onions into thin strips.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated beef into the pan and cook while stirring continuously.
Cook the beef for about 5 minutes until it begins to brown.
Add the sliced onions and carrots to the pan and stir-fry with the beef.
Continue cooking for another 3–4 minutes until the vegetables soften slightly.
Sprinkle sesame seeds and freshly chopped green onions over the dish.
Taste and adjust seasoning with a little salt or black pepper if needed.
Serve the bulgogi hot with steamed rice or lettuce wraps.

title: Samosa
steps: Begin by boiling the potatoes in a pot of water until they become soft and easy to mash.
Allow the boiled potatoes to cool slightly, peel off the skins, and mash them roughly.
Finely chop the onion, green chilies, garlic, and fresh coriander leaves.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat and add cumin seeds, allowing them to splutter.
Add chopped onions and sauté until they turn soft and translucent.
Add garlic and green chilies and cook for about one minute until fragrant.
Add the green peas to the pan and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the mashed potatoes along with coriander powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt.
Mix the filling thoroughly and cook for about 3–4 minutes so the spices blend well.
Add chopped coriander leaves and allow the filling to cool.
In a bowl mix all-purpose flour, a pinch of salt, and a little oil.
Gradually add water and knead the mixture into a firm dough.
Divide the dough into small balls and roll them into thin oval shapes.
Cut each oval in half and form a cone shape using one half.
Fill the cone with the prepared potato mixture and seal the edges with water.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan and fry the samosas until they turn golden brown and crispy.
Remove the samosas from oil and place them on paper towels to drain excess oil.
Serve hot with mint chutney or tamarind chutney.

title: Rajma Masala
steps: Start by washing the kidney beans thoroughly and soaking them overnight in plenty of water.
Drain the soaked beans and rinse them again before cooking.
Place the beans in a pressure cooker with fresh water and cook until they become soft and tender.
Finely chop the onions, tomatoes, garlic, and ginger and keep them ready for cooking.
Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat and add cumin seeds.
Allow the cumin seeds to splutter and release their aroma.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they become soft and lightly golden.
Add minced garlic and ginger and cook for about one minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they break down into a thick masala base.
Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt and mix well.
Add the cooked kidney beans along with some of the cooking water.
Stir the mixture well so the beans are coated with the masala.
Allow the curry to simmer for about 10–15 minutes so the flavors blend.
Sprinkle garam masala over the curry and stir gently.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves before serving.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
steps: Begin by cutting the chicken into medium-sized cubes and washing them thoroughly.
In a bowl combine yogurt, turmeric powder, chili powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and salt.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and mix well so every piece is coated.
Cover the bowl and allow the chicken to marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Finely chop the onions, tomatoes, garlic, and ginger while the chicken marinates.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they become golden and soft.
Add ginger and garlic and cook for about one minute until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften and form a thick sauce.
Add the marinated chicken into the pan and mix thoroughly with the masala.
Cook the chicken for about 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add a small amount of water if the sauce becomes too thick.
Stir in butter and fresh cream to create a rich and creamy gravy.
Sprinkle garam masala and mix gently so the spices blend into the sauce.
Allow the curry to simmer for another 5 minutes on low heat.
Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves before serving.
